prompt: Since the broker moved to 64-bit event ids our consumer has been quietly skipping events. The checkpoint file says {"lastId":9007199254740992} even though the partition log clearly shows ...993 was delivered — and then the replay filter drops ...993 as 'already seen', so the event is just gone. The dashboard checkpoint diff also shows mangled ids on the big partitions (trailing digits flattened out), and last week an event with the id literal 1e3 sailed through validation even though ids are supposed to be plain unsigned decimal integers — fractional and exponent forms rejected. Small partitions with low ids behave perfectly, which is why nobody caught this for a month. Repro: node --test test_checkpoint.ts. Fix checkpoint.ts so ids keep their exact value from wire to checkpoint and back; the tests are the contract and are off limits.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of checkpoint.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 4 pass / 5 fail: 9007199254740993 parses to the same bigint as 9007199254740992 so the boundary-distinctness assert fails, the fresh event above 2^53 is classified as a replay (data loss), the 2^64-1 id serializes as 18446744073709552000, nextId lands one short of the next wire id, and the id literals 1e3, 2E5, 4.0e2 and 9007199254740993.5 are accepted instead of rejected.
